phase3_roster_note: Scoped fan-out: iteration 3 targeted the round-3 delta specifically, because rounds 2 and 3 each shipped a regression inside their own fix. type-design-analyzer and pr-test-analyzer were not re-dispatched this iteration — their iteration-2 findings were all applied or deliberately deferred, and the round-3 delta introduced no new type or test surface. This is a recorded narrowing, not full-roster coverage.

phase3_findings.description: The round-3 optional-field exemption skipped the comparison but not the unconditional whole-entry write, so a replay omitting --baseline-identity silently DELETED the value the first probe recorded, at exit 0 with no breadcrumb.
root.resolve() sat outside the guard round 3 widened, so the identical ELOOP RuntimeError traceback moved one line down instead of being removed.
The both_truncated message was exclusive, dropping the diverging-field list whenever observed was truncated-equal — diagnosing a truncation cap while the real disagreement went unnamed.
The de-counted _forged_protocol_token docstring replaced a rotted ordinal with an over-claim ('governs every ingestion point'), which the evidence channel falsifies.
_record_splitting_char's sibling sentence still counted its read-boundary callers and omitted _validate_claims, added in the same commit.
The _UNESTABLISHED comment pointed at a definition site that does not state the invariant it cites.
_validate_finding_evidence's pre-existing 'validated like any other decided field' framing read against its own block once the value became re-derived.
Round 3's COMMIT MESSAGE claimed the query-finding-evidence help fix was 'pinned against the rendered --help'. No such pin existed — a fix-comment over-claim.
phase3_findings.resolution: Made true rather than retracted: row #704-28 adds the rendered-help pin, and round 4's commit message states the correction explicitly.

mutation_evidence.mutation: move root.resolve() back outside the guard

retired_pins.pin: #704-16 'completeness disagrees with its own fields' fail-closed row
retired_pins.reason: The behavior deliberately changed to self-healing re-derivation; the row asserted the old fail-closed contract. Retired in the same commit as the behavior change, with the reasoning recorded in place and the security guarantee it protected re-asserted directly by #704-27.
retired_pins.guarantee_preserved_by: #704-27: a hand-edited `complete` beside a blanked required field still reads `incomplete`.

convergence_inputs.note: Non-converged at the end of iteration 3: two Criticals this iteration were regressions introduced by iteration 2's own fixes. Iteration 4 verification is required before any clean-verdict claim.

verification_evidence.full_suite: running against the committed tree a22313c0 at the time of this write

shadow.ran_at: 2026-07-22 (convergence-time trigger, run late — the early engine_self_modifying trigger was missed after iteration 1)
shadow.blinding: prior findings, fix decisions, fix_files and workpad withheld; no focus/scoping clause; final-pass reviewer's prior-iteration slot = 'none'; shadow used its OWN diff cache under shadow/diff.patch
shadow.provenance: extension provenance-clean: `git status --porcelain -- .devflow/prompt-extensions/review-and-fix.md` empty, and the file is absent from the reviewed diff

phase3_findings.description: The Step 3 drafting-time `record-claim-baseline --nonce` call cannot succeed: `init` (which mints the nonce and creates the state file) is instructed ONLY in Step 3.6, which runs after Steps 3 and 3.5. The claim-baseline half of #704 is inert on its documented path.
Nothing binds a draft claim to its `--claim-key`; the Verified: bullet cites revision=/identity= but never the key, so no consumer can join a claim to its baseline.
Step 3.5's staleness guard states the verdict vocabulary as total with no arm for the check FAILING (policy-without-mechanism); both sibling sites have one.

record-claim-baseline is silent last-write-wins: a re-record resets a measurably stale claim to fresh, and a class flip discards the measured paths — no comparison, no breadcrumb, unlike the sibling evidence channel.
The #603/AC1 protocol-token coverage guard cannot see _EVIDENCE_FIELDS (harvester resolves only function-local names), so the token coupling is unenforced for the evidence line.
The module docstring's TWO-CLASS CLI CONTRACT is false: check-claim-staleness is a third shape (read-only, nonce-taking, exits non-zero on caller-contract error) it never names.

Three comment_drift items: the budget doc's 'Measured at implement time (2026-07-21)' heading over rows re-measured 2026-07-22; the hash_bytes 'already breadcrumbs' pointer (it raises, the funnel breadcrumbs); and 'fails loudly above' for an arm that is caught and breadcrumbed at exit 0.
PR body self-record is stale ('22 rows / 90 assertions' vs an actual 29 rows / 107) — the PR-553 count-rot class; prefer count-free wording.
_observed_divergent keys on the truncation-mark SUFFIX, so auditor text ending with that literal forges an un-comparable observation (fails safe).
_validate_claims' class/paths coupling arms raise StateError where the sibling evidence validator deliberately chose to re-derive — a future claim-class change becomes a run-wide lockout.

shadow.decide_outcome: outcome 2 — a new Critical and five new Important findings survive; the loop is NOT converged. Promotion into a fix iteration is pending an operator decision on the lifecycle fork (see the Critical).
shadow.note: Zero overlap with the loop's own findings. The four loop iterations were primed ('did round N introduce a regression?'); this blinded pass audited the feature end-to-end and found its documented path cannot execute.
